name: taskipy (Task Runner for Poetry/pyproject.toml)

performance.notes: Negligible overhead; task is subprocess execution

agent_readiness.notes: npm-scripts-style task runner for Python projects. pyproject.toml: [tool.taskipy.tasks] test = 'pytest tests/'. lint = 'ruff check src/'. build = 'python -m build'. task test → runs pytest. task lint. Pre/post hooks: pre_test = 'echo starting tests'. post_test = 'echo done'. task --list to see all tasks. Runs in current venv context. Simple string commands only (no complex task dependencies like Makefile). Alternative to Makefile for Poetry projects. Works with any package manager using pyproject.toml.

gotchas: No task dependency graph — tasks run independently
String commands only — no Python function tasks like invoke or nox
Cross-platform shell commands may differ (Windows vs Unix)
No argument passing to tasks beyond simple string appending
Limited compared to Makefile, nox, or invoke for complex workflows
best_when: Simple command shortcuts for lint, test, build, format
Teams wanting task definitions in pyproject.toml (not Makefile)
Quick project onboarding: 'task test' instead of long commands
avoid_when: Complex task dependencies or build graphs (use Makefile or nox)
Tasks needing Python logic (use invoke or nox)
summary: taskipy adds npm-scripts-style tasks to pyproject.toml. [tool.taskipy.tasks] test = 'pytest'. lint = 'ruff check src/'. Then: task test; task lint; task --list. Pre/post hooks: pre_test = 'echo start'. MIT. String commands only — no Python functions. Best for Poetry projects wanting simple task shortcuts. Use invoke or nox for complex task logic.
